intro.speaker: npc
intro.text: Oh. You're the new lead. I'd shake your hand but I've been debugging the auth service since 3 AM.

intro_2.speaker: npc
intro_2.text: Eight years I've kept this infrastructure running. Eight years of 'we'll fix the technical debt next quarter.'

assess.speaker: npc
assess.text: So. What's your plan for the platform? Please tell me it's not 'move fast and break things.'

choices.text: Tell me about the current architecture.
What keeps you up at night?
I'll figure it out. Don't worry.

architecture.speaker: npc
architecture.text: Our stack is a decade of accumulated decisions. Some good, some... 'expedient.' The core services are stable but the deployment pipeline is held together with scripts I wrote in 2019.

architecture_2.speaker: npc
architecture_2.text: If you want AI in production, we need monitoring, rollback capability, and frankly, a server room that doesn't overheat in summer.

concerns.speaker: npc
concerns.text: What keeps me up? The single point of failure in our data pipeline. The fact that our 'DR plan' is my phone number on a sticky note. The server room lock that's been broken since 2019.

concerns_2.speaker: npc
concerns_2.text: Will it run at 2 AM on a Saturday? That's my test for everything. Most things fail it.

nova_connection.speaker: npc
nova_connection.text: Nova's brilliant. Also terrifying. She deploys prototypes to production at 11 PM on Fridays. I've aged ten years since she joined.

nova_connection_2.speaker: npc
nova_connection_2.text: If we're going to run ML in production, we need guardrails. Real ones. Not 'we'll add monitoring later.'

dismissive.speaker: npc
dismissive.text: 'Don't worry.' Right. The last person who said that deployed to production without a rollback plan. We were down for 14 hours.

closing.speaker: npc
closing.text: Come back when you've got a real plan. I'll be here. I'm always here.

check_in.speaker: npc
check_in.text: Back again. Got a technical question, or just checking that the servers are still running? They are. Barely.

choices.text: I want to invest in platform reliability.
Can you help with the server room?

reliability.speaker: npc
reliability.text: You... actually want to invest in reliability? I'm not used to hearing that. Usually it's 'can you make it faster' or 'can you add this feature by Friday.'

reliability_2.speaker: npc
reliability_2.text: Give me the resources and I'll build you a platform that doesn't wake me up at 2 AM. That's all I've ever wanted.

server_room.speaker: npc
server_room.text: The server room? It's been locked since the 'incident.' Nobody talks about the incident. I have a key somewhere... but you'd need a good reason to go down there.

server_room_2.speaker: npc
server_room_2.text: If you can convince Harry it's worth the risk, I'll give you the key. The old monitoring hardware down there could be... useful.

status_update.speaker: npc
status_update.text: The usual. Three near-incidents this week, two of which I fixed before anyone noticed. The third one... let's not talk about the third one.
